   "body_snippet": "It is possible to craft a connection that executes arbitrary PowerShell commands when it is double-clicked. This is problematic because an attacker could export a code-executing connection and then trick someone into importing and clicking on it, allowing the attacker to execute code on their machine. The root of the issue is this piece of code that is used for making PowerShell connections:\r\n\r\n![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/29503289/162325436-c75e93dc-7ab3-4e49-b30d-4ccf73dc...",

   "priority": "P1-security",
   "target_release": null,
   "our_branch": null,
@@ -24,6 +24,18 @@

       "type": "triaged",
       "description": "While `SecureXmlHelper` effectively prevents XXE attacks during XML deserialization, a comprehensive audit is required to confirm that no other forms of code execution are possible through the subsequent use of deserialized data, especially in fields that may trigger external commands."

-  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): While `SecureXmlHelper` effectively prevents XXE attacks during XML deserialization, a comprehensive audit is required to confirm that no other forms of code execution are possible through the subsequent use of deserialized data, especially in fields that may trigger external commands.\nApproach: Conduct a detailed security review of how deserialized `ConnectionInfo` properties (especially those related to external commands or scripting) are handled to identify and mitigate any command injection or arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities."

   "body_snippet": "<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->\r\n\r\nMy organization uses the mRemoteng utility to remote destkop into Windows servers.\r\n\r\nThe homepage for this utility is https://mremoteng.org/.\r\n\r\nThe drives on the computer that is using the mRemoteng computer can be made available within the Remote Desktop session on the Windows servers or workstations that are being remote desktopped into by navigating to the Config section, selecting Disk Drives, and changing this option t...",
-  "our_status": "new",
-  "priority": null,
+  "our_status": "triaged",
+  "priority": "P3-enhancement",
   "target_release": null,
   "our_branch": null,
   "our_pr": null,
-  "iterations": [],
+  "iterations": [
+    {
+      "seq": 1,
+      "date": "2026-02-23",
+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "The user is reporting that local drives are not available by default for RDP connections, despite the codebase indicating 'Local' as the default setting for 'RedirectDiskDrives'. This suggests either a bug where the default is not being applied or a need for an explicit user-facing mechanism to enforce this default."
+    }
+  ],
   "comments": [
     {
       "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM5BiI5h",
@@ -97,5 +104,5 @@
   "needs_action": true,
   "waiting_for_us": false,
   "last_synced": "2026-02-21T12:06:48Z",
-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The user is reporting that local drives are not available by default for RDP connections, despite the codebase indicating 'Local' as the default setting for 'RedirectDiskDrives'. This suggests either a bug where the default is not being applied or a need for an explicit user-facing mechanism to enforce this default.\nApproach: Investigate why the 'Local' default for 'RedirectDiskDrives' might not be taking effect for new connections. This could involve examining the connection creation logic and how default settings are loaded. Depending on the root cause, the solution may involve modifying connection initialization, adding a global option in the settings UI to ensure this default, or improving how user-specific settings interact with application defaults."
 }
